title: fragaria vulgaris var. semperflorens
intro: Fragaria vulgaris var. semperflorens, also known as the strawberry tree or alpine strawberry, is a low-growing, spreading plant that produces small white flowers and edible strawberries. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant is rich in vitamins C and E, and its consumption has been associated with several health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. Fragaria vulgaris var. semperflorens is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be consumed or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
